statements.speaker: Trump
statements.quote: Let's call it quits because I've had enough, thank you, darling, have a good time.
statements.context: His exit line ending the NBC 'Meet the Press' interview with Kristen Welker after she pressed him on election-fraud and January 6 claims.

statements.speaker: Grossi
statements.speakerRole: Director General, International Atomic Energy Agency
statements.quote: Attacks on nuclear sites are completely unacceptable and in direct contravention of key nuclear safety principles.
statements.context: After a drone struck the spent-fuel storage facility near Chornobyl; Grossi called the incident deeply concerning and said the IAEA would send inspectors.

statements.speaker: Zelensky
statements.quote: This is an extremely vile attack.
statements.context: Reacting to the Russian drone strike on the Centralized Spent Nuclear Fuel Storage Facility near Chornobyl; Ukraine said radiation remained normal.

statements.speaker: Schumer
statements.quote: The American people literally cannot afford for Republicans to forgo another opportunity to work with Democrats to end Trump's disastrous war.
statements.context: Pressing Senate Republicans to allow a floor vote on the House-passed Iran war-powers resolution as energy costs rose.

statements.speaker: Trump
statements.quote: You've shot your missiles, that's enough. Get back to the table and make a deal.
statements.context: Reacting to Iran's first missile barrages at Israel since the April ceasefire, urging de-escalation and a return to talks.

statements.speaker: Trump
statements.quote: We are very close to a final deal with Iran. I'll call Bibi and tell him not to retaliate.
statements.context: Telling reporters he would press Netanyahu not to respond to Iran's missiles, claiming a deal could be signed within days.

statements.speaker: Rezaei
statements.speakerRole: Member, Iranian Parliament National Security & Foreign Policy Committee
statements.quote: Iran will give a decisive and painful response to the Zionist regime's attack on Dahieh.
statements.context: Posted on X after Israel's Beirut/Dahieh strike, hours before the IRGC missile barrage on Israel.

statements.speaker: Zelensky
statements.quote: This is a low and malicious act of terror against critical nuclear infrastructure.
statements.context: Condemning a Russian drone strike on the Chornobyl spent-nuclear-fuel storage facility.

statements.speaker: Schumer
statements.quote: We will force war-powers votes every week until Republicans see reason and help us end this war.
statements.context: Vowing repeated Senate war-powers votes after the House passed its Iran resolution.

statements.speaker: Netanyahu
statements.quote: The air force is striking a Hezbollah command center in Beirut's Dahieh that directed attacks on Israel and our troops in southern Lebanon, after the organization ignored the ceasefire and fired toward our north.
statements.context: Joint announcement with Defense Minister Israel Katz after Israel struck Beirut, days into the June 1 US-brokered ceasefire.

statements.speaker: Ebrahim Rezaei
statements.speakerRole: Member, Iran parliamentary National Security and Foreign Policy Committee
statements.quote: Iran will give a decisive and painful response to the Zionist regime's attack on Dahieh.
statements.context: Posted on X hours after Israel's June 7 strike on Beirut, framing it as a ceasefire violation and warning of retaliation.

statements.speaker: Putin
statements.quote: I do not see any provocations from Iran. If anything depends on Russia, we are always ready to offer a shoulder.
statements.context: SPIEF 2026 plenary in St. Petersburg, where Putin also declined a Zelensky meeting and touted a multipolar order led by BRICS.

statements.speaker: Bessent
statements.quote: We must let our borrowers know — U.S. households, the foreign central banks and everyone in between — that we are serious about getting our debt and deficit numbers under control.
statements.context: Senate Finance / House Ways and Means testimony defending a 3%-of-GDP deficit goal despite 5%+ budget projections and $1T+ FY2026 interest costs.

statements.speaker: Zelensky
statements.quote: It is time to end this war.
statements.context: Confirming he ordered deep drone strikes reaching about 1,000 km into Russia, hitting naval arsenals at Kronstadt near St. Petersburg, days after Putin rejected his offer for direct talks.

statements.speaker: Putin
statements.quote: Sanctions hurt those who impose them more.
statements.context: Closing the St. Petersburg International Economic Forum, arguing the West is in relative economic decline and that BRICS now drives a larger share of global growth than the G7.

statements.speaker: Trump
statements.quote: Yesterday, in a meaningless vote, the House voted, 4 bad Republicans and all of the Dumocrats, to limit my War Powers.
statements.context: Reacting to the House's 215-208 passage of an Iran war powers resolution; he also branded the four GOP defectors 'GRANDSTANDERS' and the vote 'unpatriotic.'

statements.speaker: McConnell
statements.quote: The policy threw the lives of countless immigrants living in the United States into indeterminate legal limbo.
statements.context: Ruling striking down Trump administration policies that froze asylum and immigration benefits for 39 countries.

statements.speaker: von der Leyen
statements.quote: We must make the enlargement process faster and more credible.
statements.context: Remarks at the EU-Western Balkans summit in Tivat, Montenegro, where she said Montenegro's 2028 membership goal is 'within reach.'

statements.speaker: Massie
statements.quote: People are tired of this.
statements.context: After four Republicans joined Democrats to pass the House Iran War Powers resolution rebuking Trump's strikes.

statements.speaker: Shaheen
statements.quote: You sent Congress a war powers notification saying we are not in active hostilities with Iran while the US was conducting strikes against Iran.
statements.context: Rebuking Secretary of State Rubio's claim that the Iran war is over amid continuing strikes.

statements.speaker: Blanche
statements.quote: We are not moving forward with the fund, period.
statements.context: Telling a House Appropriations subcommittee the DOJ has scrapped the roughly $1.8B 'anti-weaponization' fund; confirmed in court filings June 5.

statements.speaker: Thune
statements.quote: We don't need a weaponized DNI, we need professionals there.
statements.context: Reacting to Trump's appointment of housing chief Bill Pulte as acting Director of National Intelligence.

statements.speaker: Putin
statements.quote: I do not see any provocations from Iran.
statements.context: At the SPIEF plenary, questioning the justification for recent strikes on Iran and voicing hope the US-Iran truce becomes lasting.

statements.speaker: Trump
statements.quote: There are concepts where pieces could be given to the American public, where the American public essentially becomes a partner.
statements.context: Speaking to reporters about the possibility of the US government taking equity stakes in leading AI companies including OpenAI, Anthropic and xAI.

statements.speaker: Thune
statements.quote: It will do nothing more than fund Border Patrol and Immigration and Customs Enforcement for three years.
statements.context: Floor remarks ahead of final Senate passage of the roughly $70 billion ICE and border enforcement reconciliation bill.

statements.speaker: Lee
statements.quote: FISA 702 reauthorization failed because it did not contain a warrant requirement for spying on Americans.
statements.context: After the Senate failed to advance reauthorization of FISA Section 702, attributing the defeat to the lack of a warrant requirement rather than the controversy over Trump's DNI pick.

statements.speaker: Putin
statements.quote: What are the specific facts? What has been proven?
statements.context: Responding to an AP question at the St. Petersburg International Economic Forum after Ukrainian drones struck the city; he also vowed to bolster Russia's air defenses.

statements.speaker: Grossi
statements.quote: They seem to be pretty close to agreeing on a framework, an organizational structure to give themselves time to look into the different problems.
statements.context: Speaking after an IAEA Board of Governors meeting about the state of US-Iran nuclear talks.

statements.speaker: McConnell
statements.speakerRole: U.S. Senator (R-KY), former Senate Republican Leader
statements.quote: Anyone performing this role of such immense public trust must have the extensive national security experience required by statute, and no nominee who falls short will earn my vote.
statements.context: Warning shot after Bill Pulte was named acting Director of National Intelligence; McConnell signaled opposition to confirming him.

statements.speaker: Putin
statements.quote: I see no point in it.
statements.context: At the SPIEF plenary, rejecting Zelensky's June 4 open letter proposing a leaders' meeting and full ceasefire, saying a meeting only makes sense once a deal is ready to sign.

statements.speaker: Aoun
statements.quote: The people of Lebanon are paying the price, and they are fed up with this war.
statements.context: In a CNN interview, accusing Iran of using Lebanon as a 'bargaining chip' in its negotiations with the US and rejecting an IRGC demand to tie a ceasefire to Israeli withdrawal.

statements.speaker: Salam
statements.quote: Have mercy on our south; stop treating it and its people as merely a bargaining chip to improve the terms of your negotiations.
statements.context: Public appeal to Iran amid continued Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon, reinforcing President Aoun's criticism.

statements.speaker: Sonderling
statements.quote: The administration once again produced the best month of job creation since taking office, demolishing economists' expectations.
statements.context: Official Labor Department statement on the May jobs report showing 172,000 jobs added and 4.3% unemployment.

statements.speaker: Tuberville
statements.quote: It is beyond embarrassing that 'Republicans' continue to block the SAVE America Act.
statements.context: Reacting to four GOP senators joining Democrats to defeat the SAVE Act amendment to the immigration-funding bill.

statements.speaker: Qassem
statements.quote: We are concerned only with a comprehensive cessation of aggression, a ceasefire, and the withdrawal of Israel; this deal is a roadmap to annihilate part of the Lebanese people, a farce.
statements.context: Rejecting the US-brokered Israel-Lebanon ceasefire and pilot-zone plan as Israeli strikes continued.

statements.speaker: Aoun
statements.quote: The results of the fourth round of negotiations represent the last chance to enter into a final, comprehensive ceasefire, and each party bears responsibility if it fails to respond positively.
statements.context: Urging all parties, especially Hezbollah, to accept the Washington-brokered ceasefire terms.

statements.speaker: Zelensky
statements.quote: Enough war. Ukraine is ready to fully cease fire for the duration of talks under US monitoring, beginning diplomacy from the current front line with an all-for-all prisoner exchange.
statements.context: Open letter to Putin — his first direct public address to the Russian leader since 2022 — proposing a neutral-hosted summit.

statements.speaker: Trump
statements.quote: Yesterday, in a meaningless vote, the House voted, 4 bad Republicans and all of the Democrats, to limit my War Powers, right in the middle of my final negotiations to end the War with the Islamic Republic of Iran.
statements.context: Social-media response to the House passing the Iran war powers resolution 215-208.

statements.speaker: Schumer
statements.quote: This week, Senate Democrats will launch a coordinated effort to kill the slush fund before one cent goes out the door.
statements.context: Announcing Democrats' push to strip Trump's $1.8B 'anti-weaponization' fund during the ICE vote-a-rama.

statements.speaker: Everett Kelley
statements.speakerRole: President, American Federation of Government Employees
statements.quote: Workers who once felt comfortable reporting waste, fraud, abuse, and mismanagement because they were protected from retaliation will now be afraid for their jobs if they speak out.
statements.context: Reacting to Trump's executive order stripping firing protections from about 8,000 federal workers.

statements.speaker: Naim Kassem
statements.quote: For our fighters to leave the south while under attack would mean surrender, defeat and achieving the enemy's goals.
statements.context: Rejecting the Washington-brokered Israel-Lebanon conditional ceasefire from which Hezbollah was excluded.

statements.speaker: Joseph Aoun
statements.quote: The results of the fourth round of negotiations represent the last chance to enter into a final, comprehensive ceasefire.
statements.context: Urging Hezbollah and other parties to accept the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire reached in Washington.

statements.speaker: Abbas Araghchi
statements.quote: No tangible progress has been made in negotiations, and any Israeli attack on Beirut would trigger a full-scale resumption of the conflict.
statements.context: On the status of US-Iran ceasefire talks amid Israel's campaign against Hezbollah in Lebanon.

statements.speaker: Vladimir Putin
statements.quote: Russia will strengthen its air defenses to counter Ukraine's long-range drone strikes that have reached deep inside the country.
statements.context: Speaking after Ukrainian drones struck St. Petersburg as he hosted the SPIEF economic forum.

statements.speaker: Schumer
statements.quote: Amendment after amendment, vote after vote, Republicans are going to have to answer to the American people.
statements.context: During the Senate vote-a-rama on the roughly $70B ICE and Border Patrol funding reconciliation bill.

statements.speaker: Trump
statements.quote: We're taking historic action to bring down the price of energy and the cost of living with the power of clean, beautiful coal.
statements.context: Announcing a $700 million Defense Production Act plan to support coal plants and a California export terminal at the White House.

statements.speaker: Aoun
statements.quote: This is the last chance to reach a comprehensive truce.
statements.context: Urging acceptance of the US-brokered Israel-Lebanon ceasefire framework hours before Hezbollah rejected it.

statements.speaker: Trump
statements.quote: They should be ashamed of themselves.
statements.context: Reacting on social media to the House's 215-208 passage of the Iran war powers resolution, deriding the four Republican backers as 'grandstanders' and the vote as 'meaningless.'

statements.speaker: Thune
statements.quote: We don't need a weaponized DNI, we need professionals there.
statements.context: Signaling resistance to Pulte's acting-DNI role amid a broader Senate Republican rebellion over the appointment.

statements.speaker: Zamir
statements.speakerRole: IDF Chief of Staff (Lt. Gen.), Israel
statements.quote: In Lebanon, there is no ceasefire for our forces.
statements.context: As Israel and Lebanon announced a renewed conditional ceasefire, signaling the military intends to keep striking perceived threats in southern Lebanon.

statements.speaker: Kassem
statements.quote: Leaving southern Lebanon under fire would mean surrender, defeat and achieving the enemy's goals.
statements.context: On Hezbollah's Al-Manar TV, rejecting the US-brokered Israel-Lebanon ceasefire terms and demanding a full Israeli withdrawal.

statements.speaker: Zelensky
statements.quote: It is only a matter of time before we are able to increase the scale of our own mass strikes.
statements.context: After Ukrainian deep-strike drones hit a St. Petersburg oil terminal hours before Putin's economic forum opened.

statements.speaker: Cassidy
statements.quote: While I support the administration’s efforts to dismantle Iran’s nuclear program, the White House and Pentagon have left Congress in the dark.
statements.context: Explaining his vote to advance the Iran war powers resolution, days after losing his GOP primary.

statements.speaker: Tillis
statements.quote: Pulte does not have a path to confirmation in the Senate as a permanent director of national intelligence.
statements.context: Assessing Trump’s appointment of Bill Pulte as acting Director of National Intelligence (paraphrased).

statements.speaker: Kelley
statements.speakerRole: National President, American Federation of Government Employees
statements.quote: This is a blatant attempt to corrupt the federal government by eliminating employees’ due process rights so they can be fired for political reasons.
statements.context: Reacting to Trump’s executive order reclassifying about 8,000 federal workers as at-will.

statements.speaker: Jeffries
statements.quote: House Democrats successfully passed our War Powers Resolution today to stand up for the American people and hold Donald Trump accountable. It is now time for Senate Republicans to do the right thing.
statements.context: Statement after the House adopted a war powers resolution 215-208 directing Trump to end hostilities with Iran.

statements.speaker: White House
statements.quote: President Trump will continue to protect our national security using his constitutional authority as Commander-in-Chief while being transparent with Congress.
statements.context: Response calling the House Iran war powers resolution unconstitutional and saying it will not reach Trump's desk.

statements.speaker: Sotomayor
statements.quote: Just as Alabama doubled down on racial discrimination, the Court today doubles down on chaos.
statements.context: Dissent (joined by Kagan and Jackson) from the order letting Alabama use its Republican-favored congressional map.

statements.speaker: Zamir
statements.quote: There is no ceasefire for our forces; we will seize every opportunity to remove threats to the citizens of Israel and to our forces.
statements.context: Said as Trump announced a fresh Israel-Lebanon truce, signaling the IDF intends to keep operating in southern Lebanon.

statements.speaker: Peskov
statements.quote: Putin's plenary speech will focus mainly on economic issues and problems, both in our country and internationally.
statements.context: Previewing Putin's June 5 plenary address at the St. Petersburg forum, which opened as Ukrainian drones struck the city.

statements.speaker: Zelensky
statements.quote: We are seeing positive results from our long-range strikes against key targets deep inside Russia.
statements.context: After Ukrainian drones struck a St. Petersburg oil terminal hours before Russia's economic forum opened.

statements.speaker: Trump
statements.quote: A ceasefire there is much different than a ceasefire in other parts of the world. In that part of the world, a ceasefire is when you're shooting in a more moderate manner.
statements.context: Defending his claim that the Iran ceasefire holds despite continued cross-Gulf strikes, including an Iranian attack on Kuwait's airport.

statements.speaker: Araghchi
statements.quote: Contact with the United States has not been cut off, but no progress has been made in the negotiations.
statements.context: Speaking after the US struck Qeshm Island and Iran attacked Kuwait and Bahrain, with Tehran demanding a separate Lebanon truce.

statements.speaker: Jeffries
statements.quote: This war has already cost the American people more than $100 billion. Congress never authorized it, and it is past time we reasserted our constitutional war powers.
statements.context: During House floor debate before the war powers resolution directing withdrawal from hostilities with Iran passed 215-208.

statements.speaker: Johnson
statements.quote: The president is in the process of concluding a peace agreement, and we have to allow him the latitude to do that. This resolution would only weaken his hand.
statements.context: Opposing the Iran war powers resolution ahead of the 215-208 House vote that rebuked President Trump.

statements.speaker: Rubio
statements.quote: We're hopeful that the two sides will be able to issue a joint statement and an action plan on the track for security in Lebanon, independent from Hezbollah.
statements.context: Testifying before a congressional panel during the second day of the fourth round of US-mediated Israel-Lebanon talks at the State Department.

statements.speaker: Blanche
statements.quote: Look, we're not moving forward with the fund.
statements.context: Testifying to a House Appropriations subcommittee, signaling the Justice Department will abandon the $1.8 billion 'anti-weaponization' fund amid a GOP revolt that stalled the ICE and CBP funding bill.

statements.speaker: Sotomayor
statements.quote: The Court corrodes the rule of law by rewarding Alabama's gamesmanship and outright defiance of court orders.
statements.context: Dissent from the 6-3 unsigned order allowing Alabama to use its Republican-favored congressional map for the 2026 midterms.

statements.speaker: Netanyahu
statements.quote: If Hezbollah does not cease attacking our cities and citizens, Israel will attack terror targets in Beirut.
statements.context: Statement after a phone call with President Trump, who had urged him to call off a planned strike on Beirut, as Israel-Lebanon talks resumed in Washington.

statements.speaker: Rubio
statements.quote: Reopening the Strait of Hormuz is the predicate that opens the door to Phase 2 of the nuclear negotiations.
statements.context: Testimony to the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, his first public hearing since the Iran war began.

statements.speaker: Warner
statements.speakerRole: US Senator (D-VA), Senate Intelligence Vice Chair
statements.quote: Once again, the Trump administration has belatedly discovered the need to redo something it hastily dismantled in its first year.
statements.context: Reacting to Trump's executive order seeking voluntary federal security review of frontier AI models.

statements.speaker: Zelensky
statements.quote: If Ukraine is not protected from ballistic missiles and other missile strikes, those strikes will continue.
statements.context: After Russia's overnight barrage of 656 drones and 73 missiles killed at least 22 across Ukraine; he appealed for more US and European air defense.

statements.speaker: Rubio
statements.quote: There is the prospect Iran could negotiate aspects of its nuclear program, but it must declare the Strait of Hormuz open and pledge not to fire on commercial ships.
statements.context: Testimony to the Senate Foreign Relations Committee on the Iran war endgame and stalled talks.

statements.speaker: Schumer
statements.quote: A partisan thug with no experience in intelligence.
statements.context: Reacting to Trump's appointment of Bill Pulte as acting Director of National Intelligence.

statements.speaker: Johnson
statements.quote: I believe it is off the table for consideration.
statements.context: Remarks to reporters on Trump's proposed $1.8 billion 'anti-weaponization' fund.

statements.speaker: Zelensky
statements.quote: The next exchange will involve at least 1,000 people from each side, and we are working toward a 1,200-for-1,200 swap that could include imprisoned journalists and political prisoners held in Russia.
statements.context: After a second round of direct Russia-Ukraine talks in Istanbul produced a prisoner-swap framework but no ceasefire.

statements.speaker: Rubio
statements.quote: We have a plan — it doesn't call for that. What we want is a Gaza governed by a non-Hamas entity.
statements.context: Responding at a congressional hearing to Netanyahu's stated goal of seizing 70% of Gaza, signaling it falls outside Trump's 20-point framework.

statements.speaker: Hegseth
statements.quote: See you at SCOTUS.
statements.context: Vowing to appeal after a divided DC Circuit panel ruled the administration illegally barred currently serving transgender troops.

statements.speaker: Trump
statements.quote: You've been doing this for 47 years, and it cannot be allowed to go on any longer.
statements.context: Insisting US-Iran talks remain ongoing and pressing Tehran toward a deal despite its suspension claims.

statements.speaker: Zelensky
statements.quote: Russia has made it explicit: if Ukraine is not protected from ballistic and other missiles, these strikes will continue. Assistance from the United States in supplying missiles for Patriot systems is absolutely necessary.
statements.context: Statement after Russia's overnight barrage killed civilians in Kyiv and Dnipro, renewing his appeal to Washington and Europe for air-defense interceptors.

statements.speaker: Trump
statements.quote: Iran really wants to make a deal, and it will be a good one for the U.S.A. … Just sit back and relax, it will all work out well in the end — It always does!
statements.context: Truth Social post insisting US-Iran talks continue 'at a rapid pace' hours after Iran said it was suspending negotiations over Israel's Lebanon offensive.

statements.speaker: Araghchi
statements.quote: A ceasefire between Iran and the United States constitutes a comprehensive ceasefire across all fronts, including Lebanon. Any violation on one front shall be considered a violation across all fronts.
statements.context: Posted on X as Iran froze its indirect message exchange with Washington over Israel's Lebanon offensive and signaled a possible closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

statements.speaker: Hilton
statements.quote: If we don't get together as a party, if we don't unite, then we could have Tom Steyer and Xavier Becerra in the general election, and that is a disaster for California.
statements.context: Closing-argument appeal for Republican unity to secure a top-two runoff slot ahead of California's June 2 gubernatorial primary.

statements.speaker: Trump
statements.quote: Just sit back and relax, it will all work out well in the end - It always does!
statements.context: Truth Social post telling critics to stand down amid fresh US-Iran strikes, claiming Iran 'really wants to make a deal' — hours before Tehran said it had suspended talks.

statements.speaker: Schumer
statements.quote: If Republicans return to reconciliation, we will be ready with amendments to shut the fund down.
statements.context: Dear Colleague letter outlining Democrats' campaign to kill the DOJ's $1.776B 'anti-weaponization' fund, which Schumer called Trump's 'most brazen act of self-dealing.'

statements.speaker: Thune
statements.quote: The best way to handle it is if the administration decides to shut it down themselves.
statements.context: On the DOJ 'anti-weaponization' fund holding up roughly $70B in GOP immigration-enforcement funding past Trump's June 1 deadline.

statements.speaker: Trump
statements.quote: I couldn't care less. If they're over, they're over.
statements.context: Telling CNBC he was unbothered by reports that Iran had suspended ceasefire negotiations, adding the talks had 'started to get very boring.'

statements.speaker: Trump
statements.quote: Talks are continuing, at a rapid pace, with the Islamic Republic of Iran.
statements.context: Truth Social post contradicting Iranian state media claims that Tehran had suspended negotiations through mediators.

statements.speaker: Becerra
statements.speakerRole: Candidate for Governor of California; former US HHS Secretary and CA Attorney General
statements.quote: This is not a place for on-the-job training.
statements.context: Closing-stretch campaign appearance ahead of California's June 2 top-two gubernatorial primary, touting his decades in elected office.

statements.speaker: Araghchi
statements.quote: The ceasefire between Iran and the US is a ceasefire on all fronts, including in Lebanon; its violation on one front is a violation of the ceasefire on all fronts, and the US and Israel are responsible for the consequences.
statements.context: Statement as Israel ordered strikes on Beirut's Dahiyeh and Iran suspended US-mediated messaging over the Lebanon escalation.

statements.speaker: Netanyahu
statements.quote: Following repeated violations of the cease-fire in Lebanon by the Hezbollah terrorist organization and the attacks against our cities and citizens, I have directed the IDF, together with Defense Minister Israel Katz, to strike Hezbollah targets in Beirut.
statements.context: Statement ordering strikes on Beirut's southern suburbs after the IDF captured Beaufort Castle and advanced north of the Litani River.

statements.speaker: Warren
statements.quote: It might be hard to see from this picture of the White House today, but Donald Trump is very focused on lowering costs.
statements.context: Sarcastic post mocking Trump's affordability messaging while a UFC arena was built on the White House South Lawn.

statements.speaker: Huffman
statements.quote: You're paying more for groceries, more for gas, more for healthcare, all while Trump is spending your money on a gaudy arch, a UFC cage on the South Lawn, and tearing down the East Wing to build his ballroom.
statements.context: Criticizing Trump's spending priorities and the planned UFC event at the White House.

statements.speaker: Zelensky
statements.quote: Last night, our warriors applied Ukraine's long-range sanctions against an oil refinery in the Russian city of Saratov — that is about 700 kilometers from the front line.
statements.context: Confirming the overnight Ukrainian deep-strike on the Rosneft Saratov refinery and other targets inside Russia.

statements.speaker: Katz
statements.quote: Twenty-six years after the withdrawal, our heroic soldiers have captured Beaufort once again, and will remain there as part of the security zone in Lebanon.
statements.context: After the IDF seized Beaufort Castle and pushed beyond the Litani River in its deepest Lebanon incursion since 2006.

statements.speaker: Sherrill
statements.quote: To the people coming from out of state to create chaos and dangerous situations: You should not be here.
statements.context: At a press conference after NJ State Police took over security from ICE at the Delaney Hall detention center amid nightly protests.

statements.speaker: Cheung
statements.quote: There has been no vice president in history who has been more empowered, and that is a reflection of the strong trust and relationship between the two.
statements.context: Rebutting a New York Times report that Trump privately doubts whether Vance can lead MAGA after 2028.

statements.speaker: Trump
statements.quote: Iran's enriched uranium will be destroyed, either in the U.S., in Iran itself, or at another acceptable location.
statements.context: Weekend remarks as Trump withheld final sign-off on the US-Iran ceasefire memorandum, demanding tougher nuclear terms and warning the US would 'finish the job' if talks collapse.

statements.speaker: Araghchi
statements.quote: Talks with the United States are ongoing, but any deal cannot be judged before a definitive outcome is reached.
statements.context: Tehran maintaining that its highly enriched uranium and enrichment rights are not conceded amid the stalled US-Iran memorandum; the IAEA reported a record Iranian stockpile the same day.

statements.speaker: Pence
statements.quote: In many respects Republicans have lost our way, but Democrats have lost their mind, and I think that's the reason why we're going to hold the Senate.
statements.context: On NBC's Meet the Press Sunday, promoting his new book and discussing GOP Senate prospects after Ken Paxton's Texas primary runoff win.

statements.speaker: Pence
statements.quote: The idea of creating a fund that could compensate people who assaulted police officers and vandalized the Capitol that day is totally unacceptable.
statements.context: On CBS's Face the Nation Sunday, breaking publicly with the Trump DOJ over the $1.776B 'anti-weaponization' fund a federal judge had just blocked.

statements.speaker: Beshear
statements.quote: Texas is in play. Democrats have never run against a candidate like Ken Paxton that is so corrupt that his own party impeached him.
statements.context: On NBC's Meet the Press Sunday, after AG Ken Paxton crushed Sen. John Cornyn 63.8% in the May 26 GOP runoff and Cook shifted Texas Senate to 'Lean R.'

statements.speaker: Kim
statements.quote: I'm hugely concerned. I am so worried about my state. I have not seen my state with this level of precariousness during my entire time in elected office.
statements.context: On CNN with Dana Bash, asked whether the Delaney Hall ICE facility protests in Newark could escalate. Kim had been pepper-sprayed at the facility earlier in the week.

statements.speaker: Barrot
statements.quote: Nothing can justify the continuation of Israeli military operations in Lebanon and its ever-deeper occupation of Lebanese territory.
statements.context: Statement issued alongside France's Sunday request for an emergency UN Security Council meeting Monday on Israel's capture of Beaufort Castle.

statements.speaker: Macron
statements.quote: Nothing justifies the major escalation under way in south Lebanon.
statements.context: Statement on X after Israeli forces captured Beaufort Castle; Macron also called for an emergency UN Security Council meeting and urged a rapid US-Iran agreement.

statements.speaker: Netanyahu
statements.quote: The occupation of Beaufort is a dramatic stage and a dramatic shift in the policies we are leading.
statements.context: Statement after Israel's Golani Brigade raised the Israeli flag over Beaufort Castle in southern Lebanon — the deepest Israeli ground push into Lebanon in 26 years.

statements.speaker: Zelensky
statements.quote: Today at night, or tomorrow at night, we think that we will have big attacks from the Russian side using drones, using cruise missiles and ballistic missiles.
statements.context: Interview aired May 31 on CBS's Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan, warning of an imminent large-scale Russian aerial barrage and pressing for more Patriot interceptors.

statements.speaker: Trump
statements.quote: If you're going to be in a hurry, you're not going to make a good deal. If we don't get what we want, we're going to end it a different way.
statements.context: Comments to reporters after weekend Situation Room meetings on the proposed Iran ceasefire extension.

statements.speaker: Hegseth
statements.quote: The United States is more than capable of resuming strikes on Iran if peace talks collapse, and our stockpiles are more than suited to do so.
statements.context: Remarks at the Shangri-La Dialogue defense forum in Singapore.

statements.speaker: Pence
statements.quote: I was offended on the anniversary of January 6 when the White House put out a timeline that literally blamed Capitol Hill police for the riot.
statements.context: Interview on CBS's Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan, promoting his book on conservative principles.
